name: Grand Princess
description: Grand Princess was the world's largest cruise ship upon her 1998 debut — a title she held briefly but memorably. As the lead ship of the Grand class, she set the blueprint for a generation of Princess vessels. She offers a wide range of dining, entertainment, and relaxation options across 18 decks, including a swim-against-the-current pool and multiple specialty restaurants.

name: Diamond Princess
description: Diamond Princess was built by Mitsubishi Heavy Industries in Japan and is one of two modified Grand-class ships designed and outfitted primarily for Asian itineraries. She gained worldwide attention in February 2020 as one of the first COVID-19 quarantine ships. Today she sails Japan, Southeast Asia, and the South Pacific, offering a unique blend of Japanese hospitality and Princess's international cruising standard.

name: Sapphire Princess
description: Sapphire Princess has an unusual origin story — she was originally ordered as Diamond Princess, but caught fire during construction at Mitsubishi Heavy Industries in Japan, causing the two sister ships to swap names. She eventually joined the fleet and is outfitted for Asian and Australian markets, featuring unique dining venues and a distinctive atrium.

name: Caribbean Princess
description: Caribbean Princess is a modified Grand-class vessel built by Fincantieri with an expanded passenger capacity compared to the original Grand-class ships. She is a popular choice for Caribbean and East Coast itineraries, featuring Princess's hallmark Movies Under the Stars outdoor screen and a wide variety of dining and entertainment options.

name: Crown Princess
description: Crown Princess is the second ship to bear the name in the Princess fleet. A modified Grand-class vessel, she features an expanded Horizon Court buffet, Movies Under the Stars, and a popular Club Fusion entertainment lounge. Crown Princess is a favorite on Mediterranean and Caribbean routes.

name: Emerald Princess
description: Emerald Princess is a sister ship to Crown Princess and one of the most versatile ships in the fleet, regularly rotating between Caribbean, Mediterranean, and Northern European itineraries. She features Princess's signature Movies Under the Stars poolside screen, a well-equipped spa, and an extensive range of dining choices.

name: Ruby Princess
description: Ruby Princess is the final ship of the Grand class and gained notoriety in March 2020 when passengers disembarked in Sydney during the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic. She has since fully resumed service as a crowd favourite, sailing the Mexican Riviera, Alaska, and Caribbean, and homeporting out of Los Angeles and Fort Lauderdale.

name: Coral Princess
description: Coral Princess is the lead ship of the Coral class and was purpose-built to Panamax dimensions so she can transit the Panama Canal — a feat no larger Princess ship can perform. Built by Chantiers de l'Atlantique in France, she features distinctive decorative faux gas turbines flanking the funnel. She regularly operates Panama Canal, Alaska, and South America itineraries.

name: Island Princess
description: Island Princess is the second Coral-class ship, built to the same Panamax specification as Coral Princess and named after the iconic original Island Princess from The Love Boat era. Her manageable size and ability to transit the Panama Canal make her ideal for longer destination voyages through the Canal and around South America.

name: Royal Princess
description: Royal Princess introduced a whole new era for Princess Cruises when she debuted in 2013, christened by HRH The Duchess of Cambridge (Kate Middleton). She is the lead ship of the Royal class and features a dramatic glass-enclosed atrium, an over-the-ocean SeaWalk glass-floored walkway, and an expansive Movies Under the Stars venue.

name: Regal Princess
description: Regal Princess is the sister ship to Royal Princess and mirrors her innovations including the iconic SeaWalk — a glass-floored cantilevered walkway extending 28 feet beyond the ship's edge. She regularly homeports in Galveston, Texas, making luxury cruising accessible to the American South.

name: Majestic Princess
description: Majestic Princess was originally designed and outfitted for the Chinese-speaking market, featuring Mandarin-speaking staff, dedicated Chinese dining, and a mahjong room. She now sails global itineraries including Alaska — where she relaunched Princess's fleet post-COVID — Australia, and the Mediterranean.

name: Sky Princess
description: Sky Princess is an enhanced Royal-class ship with a larger passenger complement and additional amenities over the original trio. She debuted in October 2019 and features a new take on Princess's signature lounges and restaurants, as well as the innovative Sky Suites positioned under the ship's funnel for spectacular forward views.

name: Enchanted Princess
description: Enchanted Princess was delivered in September 2020 during the cruise industry's COVID-19 shutdown and didn't begin carrying passengers until November 2021. She is an enhanced Royal-class ship, featuring the Princess MedallionClass technology platform that enables keyless cabin entry, on-demand food and drink delivery anywhere on the ship, and personalised service throughout.

name: Discovery Princess
description: Discovery Princess is the sixth and final Royal-class ship, delivered in January 2022. She is the most technology-forward ship of the class, with the full MedallionClass suite built-in from the ground up. She homeports in Los Angeles and sails the Mexican Riviera and Alaska, making her a popular choice for West Coast cruisers.

name: Sun Princess
description: Sun Princess is the flagship of the Princess fleet and the lead ship of the revolutionary Sphere class — the largest ships ever built for Princess. Delivered in February 2024, she is the first LNG-powered Princess ship and features a dramatic atrium design called The Dome, which serves as a multi-purpose entertainment and dining hub. The Sun Princess brings an entirely new standard of luxury and technology to the line.

name: Star Princess
description: Star Princess is the sister ship to Sun Princess and the second Sphere-class vessel, delivered in 2025. She is the third Princess ship to carry this iconic name and the second LNG-powered ship in the fleet. With her maiden voyage delayed to October 2025, she joins the fleet as one of the most anticipated new ships in Princess history.
